author: @Soul-Spirit-SS
text: The speed of the wheel is only as fast as the water coming out of the jet hitting the circumference of the disk, but the torque of the wheel is speed times mass of water. If the generator (or two generator sets) is on The axial outside of the water flow the the outside diameter of the generator is governed more by the torque than speed thereby generating more watts per volume of water. Can you also use an OPEN pair of disks with a quarter only water shield (Tesla turbine) to generate watts?

author: @hldelling55delling75
text: I've been interested in the water approach for a while, now. My concern is the cost benefit ratio. Our water bill has turned into a cash cow, for the city!What about available shallow ground water wells? Is retrieving the water cost effective?

replies.author: @hldelling55delling75

author: @stevetobias4890
text: In your opinion, would a server fan rated at 12v 3a do a better job with a larger Pelton Wheel respective to the size of the larger fan?

author: @elizabethmckinney7182
text: There seems to me that the water pressure far exceeded the needs of the single fan.  Could you try a stacking modification, hooking up multiple pc fans back to back on a single shaft? In thinking you can draw way more power from that single wheel.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh for sure - this was only a model to show the principle - I am sure a better job could be done

replies.author: @elizabethmckinney7182
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering it would still be a cool video though.  I'm not 100% sure pc fans can even be done that way. Isn't there a small circuit board in the way? I may be thinking of another kind of fan I to apart.

replies.author: @DavidKenny64
replies.text: @elizabethmckinney7182 I thought there was untapped power as well.  The circuit board is in the way, as you suggested.  I thought he could replace the Pelton Wheel with a small gear and drive it with a larger gear that is on the same shaft as the the Pelton Wheel.  But he leaves that up to us.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text:
Another good video, even if a repeat from the other channel.
If I had to bet, I'd go with your "rose" turbine being as good or better than the pelton, and both might be better with an appopriately shaped shroud around them.  
If you look up the evolution of the roots supercharger, the original design was intended as a high-torque water driven turbine.  The Roots brothers, of Connersville, Indiana, were trying to use canal-water power for driving power tools and machinery, but the wooden rotors didn't work properly.  Executed in metal or other impervious material, they are still good turbines and driven compressors.  The roots was just one of many pre-steam, pre-electricity ways of getting power from river or canal water in the U.S., and predated the Tesla turbine by a couple decades.   I don't have a printer, but wonder if a roots or screw type compressor/turbine roller could be made that way.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thank you for the heads up mate - I have never heard of this and I am going to look into it for sure
